title: Heaps, Priority Queues, and Scheduling · Cost-Model Clinic: Priority Ties
summary: Schedule incidents by urgency, arrival, and changing operational priority. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dsa priority ties increment in a stable priority scheduler with heap invariants. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Add stable tie breakers without comparing unrelated payload objects.
Trace sift-up, sift-down, push, pop, replace, and heapify costs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

A defensible solution applies Priority Ties, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a stable priority scheduler with heap invariants.

content.code:
from heapq import heappop, heappush

queue: list[tuple[int, int, str]] = []
for sequence, (priority, name) in enumerate([(2, "audit"), (1, "restore"), (2, "notify")]):
    heappush(queue, (priority, sequence, name))
assert heappop(queue)[2] == "restore"
